observations.managementAction: Of the 178 prior years’ audit recommendations embodied in the previous years’ Annual Audit Report, 16 recommendations were merged with the other recommendations in view of the similarities of issues involved. Of the remaining 162 audit recommendations, 82 were implemented and 80 were unimplemented.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous audit recommendation and Management agreed that the inventory team composed of representatives from the CAO and GSD to: (a) prepare and update the property records such as PPELCs and PCs; and (b) reconcile the balances of PPE in the books and RCPPE and observe the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 13, 2020 on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.
We further recommended and Management agreed to assign dedicated personnel to focus on completing the preliminary activities prior to the conduct of the inventory taking, such as updating of the CAO and GSD records, in order fully avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended and the City Mayor, through the City Accountant, agreed to reclassify the purchased parcels of land to Road Networks account to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and Management agreed to exhaust all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances especially from those who were already separated / retired from the service or no longer connected with the City, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted, following the guidelines and requirements provided under COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 within one year from its effectivity.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Treasurer for the reconciliation of the subsidiary ledger and the cash book balances as at December 31, 2023 of the CIB- LCCA in the GF, SEF and TF.

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, coordinate with the City Assessor’s Office and City Treasurer’s Office for the reconciliation of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts in order to eliminate the negative or abnormal balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit an explanation pertaining to these disbursements without the required appropriation ordinance, for the Audit Team’s evaluation.
We also recommended and Management through the City Accountant agreed to recognize as income the funds received without specific purpose, and make them available for appropriations in accordance with law.

We recommended and Management, through the City Treasurer, agreed to intensify its tax collection campaign for RPT and if warranted, enforce the appropriate remedies as provided in R.A. No. 7160 or in the City’s Revenue Code.

We recommended that Management prioritize the registration, transfer and titling of these parcels of land to ensure its ownership and protect the interest of the City in compliance with Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.
We further recommended that Management submit the supporting documents on the payment of Capital Gains Tax and Documentary Stamp Tax amounting to ₱4,161,234.30 and amounting ₱881,881.65, respectively, including an update on the status of the registration of the lots pertaining thereto to the Audit Team, for review and verification.

The City Accountant revise the Report on Utilization of DRRM Fund and include previous year’s appropriation transferred to the Special TF to reflect total funds available for proper planning and decision-making pertaining to the utilization of the said fund.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the City for these achievements and recommended that
Management continue to improve local revenue generation, through appropriate
collection strategies and innovations that are in accordance with Local Government
Code and pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the inventory team
composed of representatives from the CAO and GSD shall: (a) prepare and update
property records such as PPELCs and PCs; and (b) reconcile the balance of PPE in
the books and RCPPE.

Further, we recommended that Management observe the procedures in the conduct
of the physical count as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 13, 2020
on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to review
and account for the details of the CIP accounts with no SLs, unclear project name,
and SL with negative balance so as to present fairly the financial statements of the
City as at a given period.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accounting Office to prepare
the Journal Entry Voucher to record the donated PPEs and maintain a separate
PPE Ledger Card for these donated PPEs. We also recommended that Management
conduct an appraisal on donated PPEs with no declared value and submit the
appraisal report to the City Accounting Office to be used as a basis in recording in
the books of the City.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Accountant and the
City Treasurer to jointly reconcile the subsidiary ledger and cash book balances as
at December 31, 2021 of the CIB- LCCA in the General, Special Education and
Trust Funds.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to exhaust all possible remedies to
collect and to demand liquidation of cash advances especially from those who were
already separated from the service, otherwise, request for an authority to write-off
from COA, if warranted.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Legal Office to take
appropriate action to:

a. Recover the amount of the 15 per cent advance payment granted to the
contractor for the two projects, and claim the Performance Bond for the
partially finished school building including the two projects that were not
started by the contractor;

b. Terminate, in whole and in part, the contract entered into with Bunhua
Construction Inc. pursuant to Article IV of Annex I of the Revised IRR of R.A.
9184 on the procedures for termination of contracts; and

c. Blacklist the contractor in accordance with Section 6.0 of Appendix 17 of the
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 on the procedure for blacklisting during the contract
implementation stage.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development
Officer and City Engineer to do away with the current practice of solving land
issues during the project implementation phase and follow the Detailed Engineering
guidelines pursuant to Annex A of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, in order to achieve
on time the completion of projects for ready use by its constituents to positively
promote its governance.

We recommended that the Toledo City Government establish a CPES-IU that will
implement the CPES implementing guidelines to provide information on the
performance of constructors in government projects and reap the benefits of timely
completion of infrastructure projects with the desired quality, among others.

We recommended that Management, through the BAC, submit to the Audit Team
the FDA’s License to Operate as drug retailer/wholesaler/distributor for the above-
mentioned supplier in order to regularize this procurement transaction. Henceforth,
we recommended that Management comply with the pertinent law, rules and
regulations in the procurement of goods/services including the related issuances of
the GPPB to ensure that contracts are awarded to eligible suppliers who have
acquired the appropriate licenses.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through the BAC
Secretariat to submit to the GPPB the prescribed reports as provided in GPPB
Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended that Management require the contractor to post another
performance security that is valid until the issuance by the City of the Certificate of
Final Acceptance.

We recommended that Management immediately implement the projects and
disburse the funds intended for the benefit of the City and its constituents if still
viable, or return the funds to the SA so that it can be used for other relevant
undertakings/purpose/s.

We recommended that Management immediately remit to the National Treasury
the unutilized PDAF amounting to ₱5,820,000.00 pursuant to the afore-cited SC
decision.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking supporting documents and
explain why such procedures were adopted instead of releasing the financial
assistance through checks payable to the Barangays for them to issue Official
Receipts to properly account and report the expenses to be paid out of the said
financial assistance.

We recommended that the Management direct the City Legal Office in coordination
with the GSD to give priority for the registration of these 281 lots in the name of the
Toledo City Government under the Torrens Title System. In the alternative, we
recommended for the creation of Land Titling Committee to intensify/ expedite the
titling efforts of the City.

We recommended that Management initiate the process of recruitment to fill in the
mandatory and other vacant positions with the appropriate qualifications so that
efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained.

Further, Management is enjoined to observe the rules and regulations on designation
as mandated by the CSC.

We recommended that Management require the HRMO to:

a. Adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other
payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any City
employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of ₱5,000.00; and

b. Direct those in charge of payroll preparation to exercise diligence in monitoring
the net take home pay of City personnel to comply with the requirements of
existing law. Remedial measures should be done to rectify the situation of the
257 City personnel receiving below the required net take home pay.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the required distribution
lists duly acknowledged by the beneficiaries to validate and account for the meals
served during the said activity. We also recommended that, henceforth, the City
Accountant ensure that the payment of food assistance or packed meals are
supported with duly acknowledged distribution list.

We recommended and Management agreed to exert more effort to comply with the
GAD mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management give utmost priority to the remittance of all the
premium contributions and loan amortization payments of LGU personnel.

Also, we recommended that Management make representation with the GSIS for a
reconciliation of loan accounts to settle the differences and to establish the correct
amount that should be paid or remitted and conduct further analysis and
monitoring of the account so that necessary adjustments be recorded to achieve the
fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the City Accounting Office to
withhold the prescribed taxes from all its payments of professional/talent fees in
accordance with Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98 of the BIR as amended by RR No. 11-
2018. Further, we recommended that the amount of taxes not withheld be refunded
and remitted to the BIR.

observations.recommendation:
The City of Toledo establish an IAS Office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, National Guidelines on Internal Control System (NGICS) and Section 124 of P.D. 1445.

Management install a sound system of internal control to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, improve the quality and quantity of outputs and enable agencies to better respond to the requirements of the public they serve.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Due to COVID-19 pandemic the Office of the City Mayor needed to prioritize the resolution of the situation. The City Mayor will designate personnel to function as the City Internal Audit as per SP Resolution No. 2433-2017. The Internal Audit Service is not yet organized as required under AO No. 70 dated April 14, 2003.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 In compliance, the GAD-Focal Point System (GFPS) has been reconstituted as per Executive Order No. 14, series of 2019 dated 8/19/2019. The GAD database development is on-going. Formulation of GAD Code is still in process.

observations.recommendation:
The management direct the different implementing offices to execute all PPAs planned for implementation during the year and to submit accomplishment reports on a monthly or quarterly basis to the City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC). Advise CPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation and submit periodic reports to the City Mayor incorporating issues and concerns affecting execution of projects copy furnished our office.

Additionally, the CPDC should schedule the implementation of PPAs in coordination with the implementing offices by apportioning the number of projects to be implemented within the four quarters of the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 The 2018 Administrator submitted a letter dated 9/10/2020 detailing the various accomplishment status of the LGUs project implementation. Periodic reports of accomplishment incorporating issues and concerns affecting the execution of projects were not submitted to the CPDC. The CPDC noted that the different implementing agencies did not submit accomplishment reports to the CPDC.
observations.reason: The different implementing agencies did not submit accomplishment reports to the CPDC; periodic reports copy furnished the Audit Team was not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Management should instruct the HRMO to strengthen the internal control system in the hiring of JOs. Require all JOs to enroll in the biometric system to ensure compliance with the required number of hours of work. Adopt the use of ATM in the payment of wages to JO personnel to strengthen internal control on cash management and eliminate the risk of loss of payroll funds in the withdrawal and transporting of large sum of money.

Management should refrain hiring of JO as security guard to secure the various offices and properties of the city and instead hire a security agency with licensed security guards. Conduct competitive bidding in selecting the security agency to adhere with the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184.

Management should document the hiring of JOs by executing contract or Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the authority of the Local Chief Executive to sign the contract and recommend to the Local Sanggunian to pass a resolution that will serve as policy guidelines in the screening, recruitment and assignment of JO personnel to establish the required qualification and necessity of its services.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 The City Vice-Mayor already issued his office memorandum dated 8/10/2021 requiring Biometric System attendance on all SP job order employees except for those personnel assigned in the field. The City Mayor’s endorsement to SP for a resolution on ATM enrollment using the biometric system. Copy furnished the HRMO and City Treasurer. The City officials were advised not to tamper or revise POs, a contract or agreement was made as supplement/addendum to the PO to protect the best interest of the City. During the ECQ, it is difficult to process the documents and bring them to the signatories, like, authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the Mayor to sign the contract. During COVID-19, those projects already bidded were temporarily set aside because the relief activities was prioritized. Also, the PhilGEPs website was down during pandemic period.
observations.reason: Some offices have not yet submitted the corresponding endorsement and payroll adjustments; some practices continue while corrective measures were being implemented.

observations.recommendation: Management should require immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to avoid further penalties and instruct the accountant to regularly check whether the amount of withholding taxes remitted would reconcile with the amount of the taxes withheld for the same period. Likewise, we further recommended that the City Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of the balance of the Due to BIR account to see to it that all taxes withheld for the month are included in the remittance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 The City Accounting Office are still reconciling the accounts since the balance pertains to prior years transactions. Adjustment of errors will be made once the reconciliation is finished.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the collection of the audit disallowance by withholding the salaries or other compensation due to the persons liable who are still employed with the City. observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Accountant to submit the complete set of financial statements prescribed in PPSAS to provide information about the City which are necessary for accountability and for decision-making purposes.

We recommended the submission of the lacking supporting documents pursuant to P.D. No. 1445 and Item 4.2, Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, and henceforth, management should strictly comply with Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445 by requiring complete documentation of the transactions before effecting payment thereof.
We recommended that the City Accountant effect the following adjusting entries based on the reconciliation made on the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts as follows:

We recommended that the Treasury Department and the Office of the City Accountant personnel coordinate closely in the preparation of monthly reconciliation statements and classify all reconciling items identified in order to arrive at the correct balances of the Cash in Bank per cash book and per accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to: (a) communicate with the RCBC Head Office and follow up on the details and status of its bank account; (b) confirm with LBP Regional Office or Head Office as to the status of the City’s bank account; (c) secure relevant documents pertaining to the Cash-in-Bank – LCSA and make necessary adjustments to properly take up any identified reconciling items and furnish the audit team with the related Journal Entry Voucher/s together with the pertinent supporting documents.

We recommended that the City submit to this office all contracts together with its supporting documents within five (5) calendar days upon approval for timely technical review and evaluation of compliance with laws, rules and regulations and completeness of documentary requirements and for timely management action on deficiencies noted pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by strengthening control measures to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents. Issue written notice to demand settlement or file Gross Neglect of Duty for failure to render the account in full within the
prescribed period pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12, series 2012 re- amendment to Section 8, Rule V of CSC Resolution No. 04-0676 dated June 17, 2004.

We recommended that the City Accountant segregate the cost of road asset components for each road network and compute depreciation based on each road component except for the road lot for proper valuation and recording in the Financial Statements.
Also, we recommended that management follow the accounting and reporting guidelines in the recognition of the city’s road network and properly identify its various components for posting in the ledger. The City Accountant, City GSO and City Engineer must coordinate properly and perform their respective duties and responsibilities outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Lastly, we recommended that the City Accountant prepare adjusting entries to recognize the four (4) road network recorded in the inventory totaling
₱48,437,826.73;verify the exact valuation of the two (2) road networks having different amount in the books and inventory report; and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommended that management follow the minimum standards and requirements for the collection of solid wastes and provide tarpaulin cover to all dump trucks transporting the city’s garbage. Ensure that personnel directly dealing with the collection of solid wastes be fully equipped with personal protective equipment to protect them from the hazards of collecting wastes.
We also recommended that the Executive Order be amended to conform with the required composition of the City SWM Board pursuant to Section 12 of
R.A. 9003.

We recommended to management the following:
Make the following adjusting entries in the books of account of the City of Toledo:
Send demand letters to these delinquent stall occupants compelling them to immediately settle their balances.
We also recommended that management submit the lease contract agreements of the occupants of the market stalls together with an updated report providing all the necessary information to facilitate computation of the exact rental to be collected.

We recommended that henceforth, management integrate the CSHP in the computation of the construction cost of all projects to be implemented by the City and require contractors to submit the CSHP duly approved by DOLE as required under the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to form part of the contract.

We recommended that the City Accountant properly scrutinize the claims for honoraria of the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG to avoid double claim for the same procurement project and submit the following documents to support their claim of honoraria for the periods listed above as follows:
Minutes of BAC Meeting
Certification that the procurements involved competitive bidding.

We recommended that all infrastructure projects undertaken by the City of Toledo must have a suitably framed project signboards in accordance with the specifications in COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 containing complete information for the public to gain access of information on matters of public concern. Direct contractors should repair any damaged signboards and keep its information updated.
Moreover, we also recommended that the Head of the Agency inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the issuance of Notice of Award or before the start of the project that the project signboards were already posted at the venue of the project for monitoring purposes pursuant to COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the City of Toledo shall establish an IAS Office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of P.D. 1445.
We also recommended that Management install a sound system of internal control to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, improve the quality and quantity of outputs and enable agencies to better respond to the requirements of the public they serve.
We recommended that the BAC Chairperson refrain from notarizing the approved contract of the City of Toledo.

We recommended that management prepare a status report on the whereabouts of the Work/Other Animals and Breeding Stocks and require the City Agriculture Officer to confirm the existence of these assets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to closely monitor the changes of the total amount of monthly contribution for remittance intact and to trace the breakdown of prior year’s balances for reconciliation and adjustment.
We recommended that the City Accountant effect the following adjusting entry in the SEF books:

We recommended that the LDRRMO submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of Local DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to reflect in the said report the transactions for the current appropriation of the DRRMF for proper evaluation and monitoring.

We recommended that Management administer the HGDG Project Design Checklist during the GPB Preparation and facilitate the PIMME checklist to determine the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget.

We recommended that the City of Toledo revisit R.A. No. 9710 also known as the MCW Act and PCW-DILG- DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 for strict compliance in the localization of the Magna Carta of Women and submit documents to this Office evidencing compliance thereof.

We recommended that the City Engineer prepare an As-Built-Plan on the Change Order reflecting the cross-sectional detail and location of items Stone Masonry, PCCP, Reflectorized Thermoplastic Pavement Marking (white and yellow) for proper evaluation on the technical aspect of the project. Henceforth, submit the documents necessary in the preparation of the Variation Order (Change Order or Extra Work Order).

We recommended that management direct the different implementing offices to execute all PPAs planned for implementation during the year and to submit accomplishment reports on a monthly or quarterly basis to the City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC). Advise CPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation and submit periodic reports to the City Mayor incorporating issues and concerns affecting execution of projects copy furnished our office. Additionally, the CPDC should schedule the implementation of PPAs in coordination with the implementing offices by apportioning the number of projects to be implemented within the four (4) quarters of the year.

We recommended that management instruct the City Human Resource Management Officer to strengthen the internal control system in the hiring of JOs. Require all JOs to enroll in the biometric system to ensure compliance with the required number of hours of work. Adopt the use of ATM in the payment of wages to JO personnel to strengthen internal control on cash management and eliminate the risk of loss of payroll funds in the withdrawal and transporting of large sum of money.
We also recommended that management should refrain hiring of JO as security guard to secure the various offices and properties of the city and instead hire a security agency with licensed security guards. Conduct competitive bidding in selecting the security agency to adhere with the provisions of the IRR of R.A. 9184.
Lastly, document the hiring of JOs by executing contract or MOA with the authority of the Local Chief Executive to sign the contract and recommend to the Local Sanggunian to pass a resolution that will serve as policy guidelines in the screening, recruitment and assignment of JO personnel to establish the required qualification and necessity of its services.

We recommended that management require the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to avoid further penalties and instruct the accountant to regularly check whether the amount of withholding taxes remitted would reconcile with the amount of the taxes withheld for the same period. Likewise, we further recommend that the City Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of the balance of the Due to BIR account to see to it that all taxes withheld for the month are included in the remittance.

We recommended that the City Assessor update the SMVs covering the different classes of real property for approval of the City Mayor and endorse the approved and updated SMV to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the enactment of appropriate ordinance.

We recommended that the City Accountant and other persons responsible submit the year-end financial reports not later than February 14 of each year. Otherwise, cause the immediate suspension of payment of their salaries pursuant to Section 122 (2) of P.D. 1445.
We further recommended, that Management in consultation with the accounting personnel, identify the cause/s of delay in finalizing the year-end reports for them to work together towards a suitable and a workable solution to this perennial problem of the City of Toledo.
Henceforth, Management should plan and set specific timelines for the year-end closing of accounting books to allow ample time for the preparation of the year-end  financial statements and supporting schedules to meet the deadlines for submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant and other persons responsible submit the year-end financial reports not later than February 14 of each year.  Otherwise, cause the immediate suspension of payment of their salaries pursuant to Section 122 (2) of P.D. 1445.

We further recommended, that Management in consultation with the accounting personnel, identify the cause/s of delay in finalizing the year-end reports for them to work together towards a suitable and a workable solution to this perennial problem of the City of Toledo.

Henceforth, Management should plan and set specific timelines for the year-end closing of accounting books to allow ample time for the preparation of the year-end financial statements and the supporting schedules to meet the deadlines for submission.

We recommended that Management institute appropriate sanctions against those employees whose cash advances has remained unliquidated to date, despite final demand thereof.

We further recommended that the City Government issue demand letters to all concerned parties including those who are no longer in service to settle pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002, all unliquidated cash advances.

Lastly, we recommended that the City Accountant file a request for authority to write-off unliquidated dormant cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.

We recommended that the Ordinance be amended to conform with the required composition of the City Waste Management Board pursuant to Section 12 of R.A. 9003.

We also recommended that the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan be formulated and submitted to the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) and mobilize the City Solid Waste Management Board created as required under R.A. 9003 to ensure quality solid waste management operations.
We recommended that Management submit the contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from execution or issuance thereof.

We recommended that Management prepare the LCPC plans and budget and adhere to  DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-121 dated July 4, 2012 and EO No. 39 Series of 2018 dated August 20, 2018 particularly Section 3 thereof.

We recommended that the City Accountant conduct verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivable accounts. If such receivables are uncollectible, the Head of the Agency shall file a request for authority to write off dormant receivable accounts and fund transfers in accordance with the guidelines required under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the agency conduct periodic physical inventory and reconciliation of property, plant and equipment by type which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year.

Likewise, we also recommended that the Management direct the City Accounting Office to maintain Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of assets to classify/identify the PPEs by type to facilitate the reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements for the period October to December 2018 and henceforth submit monthly the said statements within ten (10) days from receipt of bank statements.

We further recommended that Management make representations with the depository bank for the timely submission of bank statements.
Fill-up the four (4) vacant plantilla positions immediately to avoid disruption in the performance of achieving its program.

observations.recommendation:
Management Comments:
The management is still have to review the validity of the reconciling items since most of them incurred from prior years. The personnel in-charged already asked a copy of the prior year bank statements from the bank involved.

We recommend that the Management:
Effect necessary adjustments for the deposit in transit and unposted deposits which were only recorded in the Cash-in-Bank subsidiary ledgers but deposited in the bank under different bank accounts of the City.
Prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher for the cancellation of the stale checks.
Submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the copy/ies of the Bank Statements and supporting documents for the reconciling items within the period set forth under COA Circular No. 96-011 for a timely review of such report and immediate adjustments could be made.

Management Comments:
The management already prepared the documents for write-off for some items mentioned above for City Accountant’s review.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to conduct a thorough verification of the dormant items and request for write-off of the accounts with the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. We further recommend that the City Accounting Office conduct a regular review of all account balances so that dormant accounts can be immediately identified and acted upon.

Management Comments
The management have started reconciling the said accounts and proper adjusting entries will be effected before the end of CY 2018.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommend that the Management explain the variance of P8,507,833.06 in the inventory balance as of December 31, 2017 and trace the causes of the variance. We also recommend that the General Services and Accounting Departments coordinate and reconcile their records to support the 2017 inventory transactions and to establish the correct inventory account balances.

Management Comments
Management concur with the observation and will exert more effort in requiring submission of liquidation reports as basis of closing the balance of Due to Other NGAs account to the extent of the amount in the approved liquidation report.

We recommend that the Management:
Revisit the project proposals thru its implementing offices or departments to check the implementation of projects of these trust funds pursuant to the law governing said grant and/or the Memorandum Agreement/contract with the respective donors;
Require the proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor;
Give priority to the reconciliation of the records to establish the correct amount of the remaining PDAF; and
Return any amount of unused PDAF of legislators to the National Treasury since the use thereof was already found unconstitutional by the Supreme Court.

Management Comments
The City Accountant commented that efforts will be employed to enable them to submit the said reports on time.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to submit the complete set of financial statements prescribed in PPSAS to communicate information about the City that is useful for accountability and for decision-making purposes.

Management Comments:
The programs/projects/activities in the Annual Procurement Plan were already categorized into three major components.

We recommend that in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan, prioritized Programs/Project/Activities should be properly categorized into three major components pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommend that the City implement the projects embodied in the AIP within the time frame set forth in the Annual Procurement Plan and identify the reasons for the non-implementation of the programs and consequently make the necessary actions to facilitate implementation for benefit its intended beneficiaries/constituents.

We recommend that the management reorganize the creation of the Local Council For Protection of Children (LCPC) pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No.  2005-007.
We recommend that the management prepare the required LCPC Plan and Budget as well as the Accomplishment Reports, Monitoring Reports and Assessment & Rating Reports using the DILG Guidelines, MC No. 2005-007.

Management Comments:
The management already submitted the SEF budget for the CY 2018 to the city budget officer for review and ascertained that the budget were in conformity with the said JMC.

We recommend that the preparation and approval of SEF Budget should observed strictly the timelines and format as  prescribed under JMC No. 1, s. 2017.
We recommend that the LSB maximize the utilization of SEF Fund, implement and monitor the implementation of PPAs in order not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of the said PPAs.

We recommend that the Local School Board post the SEF Utilization Report in the website of the LGU and/or in at least three (3) conspicuous public places for transparency and accountability pursuant to Section 6.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 s, 2017 of Department of Education (DEPED), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG).

We recommend that Management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit the mandated report pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 to enable COA to perform its mandated duties and functions to validate its accomplishments.

Management Comments:
Funds for the four (4) positions were already included in the 2018 Annual Budget. Filling up of the position will take place once budget is approved.

We recommend that Management fill-up the four (4) vacant plantilla positions immediately to avoid disruption in the performance of achieving its program.

We recommend that management should observed strictly the timeliness in the preparation and submission of GAD Plan and Budget pursuant to the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-001 dated January 12, 2016 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommend that Management strictly observe the preparation and submission of GAD Plan and Budget and its utilization thereof in order to attain the GAD mandated programs/activities/projects embodied in the Magna Carta of Women pursuant to the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2012-01 of the PCW, NEDA and DBM.

We recommend that management require the LGU GAD Focal Point to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report (GAD AR) in consonance with the prescribed form and procedures pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend that the City strictly comply the provisions of Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 in the preparation of the APP.

Also, we recommend that the City submit a copy of the approved APP to GPPB and APP-CSE to DBM-PS to speed up the procurement process and to determine the quarterly cash requirements for common supplies, materials and equipment of the agency and for the over-all management of the central procurement of common-use supplies and equipment by the DBM-PS.

We further recommend that the City require the BAC to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated and approved PPMP and APP yearly for validation that all procurement undertaken by the City were based on the approved annual procurement plan.

Management Comments:
In CY 2018, the Management already started to purchase the common used-supplies from the DBM. Canvass was made for those office supplies which are not available in the PS-DBM.

We recommend that the City submit an explanation why it did not procure the goods in bulk purchases in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of R.A. 9184.
Also, we recommend that the City procure the common-use goods, supplies, materials and equipment from the Electronic Catalogue in the PhilGEPS. However, if the items are not available, we recommend that the procurement shall be done quarterly, in bulk purchase and through public bidding to avail the lower prices and purchase discounts usually given in bulk purchases.
Furthermore, we recommend that the Management observe strictly the policies and procedures under revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of common-use supplies and equipment.

We recommend that the Management stop the practice of splitting in the purchase requisitions or contracts and other related transactions pursuant to COA Circular No. 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government.

Further, we recommend that the Management submit an explanation on why the subject procurement appeared to have been split.

Management Comments:
The Management recognized and tried to explore all of these problems in order to address them.

We recommend that management enforce the collection of guarantee deposit/bond and the advance three (3) months rental fee of stalls/tarima prior to the signing of the Lease Contract Agreement in pursuance to the Revised Market Code of Toledo City.

We recommend that management enforce the execution of duly notarized Lease Contract Agreements of all existing stall/tarima holders without Lease Contracts and to specify the exact amount of rental fee to be paid  by the stallholders.

We recommend that management formulate a proposed Ordinance to establish Manual of Operation Policy to regulate the operations and maintenance of Toledo Land Transportation Terminal and to submit the same to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for approval.
We also recommend that management improve the physical facilities of the bus terminal to be more attractive for potential tenants/occupants in order to generate more resources for the City.

We recommend that the Management:
Prepare and submit the duly certified copy of the Job Order Contracts/Service Contracts by the HRMO with the statement of the expected work or output;
Use punch cards/biometrics to record the time/man-hours rendered or in its absence, the attendance logbook.
Limit the hiring of JO workers to actual number needed for normal operations of the City. Department heads should be able to provide reasonable explanation on the need to hire number of employees under contracts/ job orders.

Management Comments:
Demand letters were already sent to those accountable officers. For those who are still in the service, salary deduction has been implemented.

We recommend Management to enforce the collection of the audit disallowance by withholding the salaries or other compensation due to the persons liable who are still employed with the City.  For those officials and employees who are already separated from the government service, management should employ all possible means provided by law for the settlement of their obligations.

Demand the submission of liquidation reports and require the immediate return of unused funds, if any. Refrain from granting additional financial assistance without having previous releases liquidated first, to ensure proper fund utilization and secure government funds from misuse if not, loss.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the City Accountant to adjust the
reconciling items to establish the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account.
Henceforth, we recommend that the City Accountant prepare and submit the BRS
for all bank accounts to the Office of the Auditor on time pursuant to COA Circular
No. 96-011.

We recommend that the City Accountant conduct analysis and validation of the
existence of the receivables. If such receivables are uncollectible, the Head of the
Agency shall file a request for authority to write off dormant receivable accounts
and fund transfers in accordance with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2016-
005.

We recommend that Management demand the submission of liquidation reports
and require the immediate return of unused funds, if any. Refrain from granting
additional financial assistance without having previous releases liquidated first, to
ensure proper fund utilization and secure government funds from misuse if not,
loss.

We recommend that the City require the President of the Liga ng mga Barangay to
submit liquidation documents to establish the validity and regularity of the
utilization of the funds transferred in accordance with the provision of COA
Circular No. 94-013.

We recommend that the City implement the projects embodied in the AIP and
identify the reasons for the non-implementation of the programs and consequently
make the necessary actions to facilitate the implementation of the
programs/projects/ activities.

We recommend that Management require the City’s Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head
of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and
Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar
days after the end of each semester.

We recommend that the City implement the BuB projects within the agreed
stipulations and time frame pursuant to the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)
entered into and DBM-DILG-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated
November 3, 2015.

observations.recommendation: Direct the accountant to submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements, credit/ debit memos and paid checks for all the depository accounts of the City within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to determine the correctness of the Cash in Bank balance appearing in the financial statements and to avoid the filing of appropriate administrative charges to responsible personnel.
observations.managementAction: AAR BRS are not yet complete as of March 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the contractor/ supplier to fast track the completion of the project and enforce collection of unliquidated damages for every day of delay to be reckoned from the day after the expiration of the contract period or rescind the contract, if warranted pursuant to the Contract of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: AAR Filed case against Geodata for Rescission plus damages.
observations.reason: Implementation of recommendations is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to maintain the PPELC for all PPE accounts to facilitate reconciliation of balances between Accounting and General Services Office records. The City should conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances to come up with the accurate balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR Out of P194,012,461.60 unreconciled difference between Accounting Records and Inventory Report, 2005 P165,018,735.20 or 85.06% were already substantiated per letter received by COA last June 31, 2016. Per GSD - P1,320,323,508.87. Per Accounting Record: P1,400,815,128.11. Difference P19,333,049.36 were substantiated. Thus, the reconciled amount is 184,351,784.56 or 95.02%.

observations.recommendation: Demand the officers and employees the immediate settlement of all their cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002. Also, we recommend that Management institute appropriate sanctions prescribed in COA Circular NO. 2012-04 against those employees whose advances as of December 31, 2011 remained unliquidated to date, despite final demand as stated under Section 6 thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR Continued deducting from the salaries of active employees with unliquidated cash advances and sending demand letters to those who are not in active service.

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with DOF Circular No. 001-2015, as amended by Circular No. 002-2015 by transferring all funds deposited at PVB to its regular depository, the Land Bank of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: AAR There is an outstanding contract between the LGU and Philippine Veterans Bank (PVB). The bond floatation for the construction of the Toledo City hotel is covered by a Trust Indenture entered into by and between PVB and LGU-Toledo. The agency was able to transfer P350,000,000.00 deposit from PVB to Land Bank of the Philippines. However, due to the outstanding contract between the LGU and PVB, the agency cannot totally closed the said account.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accounting Office to coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the City Engineering Office to comply with the requirements of the foresighted laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR Substantiated 96.52% of the variance with JVs and SLs as follows: CIP- Roads, P45,358,952.41; CIP-Waterways, P628,735.78; CIP-Reforestration- P43,500.00; CIP-Parks, Plazas, P7,183,944.14; CIP-Irrigations, P61,005.00; CIP-Flood Controls P228,156.80.

observations.recommendation: Submit their explanation for non-adherence/compliance to Sec. 2 and Sec. 17.2 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in the conduct of public bidding. Produce documents necessary to show that bidding documents were purchased by prospective bidders as well as certified true copies of their bid security to ensure that a public bidding was conducted. Submit all the required documents relative to the computerization project of the City.
observations.managementAction: AAR Filed Appeal before COA Region for NS issued. Also, case has been filed against Geodata. Documents were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Implementation is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Implement the Projects/ Programs/ Activities (P/P/A) embodied in the SEF Annual Budget in order not to deprived the intended beneficiaries of the said P/P/A as we all know the importance of education.
observations.managementAction: AAR Carried over to LSB budget for 2016. Implementation is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the submission of weekly consolidated Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) as well as the preparation and recording of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) in order that the actual monthly expenditures can be reflected in the books of accounts. Require the General Services Office and Accounting Division to conduct a joint and immediate inventory taking and reconciliation to determine the cause/s of the material discrepancy noted during the physical count and effect the necessary adjusting entries to present correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR For the year 2016, weekly and monthly preparation and submission of SSMI to Accounting and COA. Reconciliation of balances between GSO and Accounting office is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Expedite the disposal of the said properties through sale or transfer without cost to other government agencies that can still make use of it before it will become totally useless. After the disposal, record the reduction of the cost of disposed property in the books of account to reflect the correct account balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR (no specific action text provided in table cell).

observations.recommendation: Prepare and submit the prescribed monthly report on official travels, properly accomplished trip tickets and the fuel consumption reports in order to determine the reasonableness of the fuel consumed during calendar year 2014 and the ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: AAR Ongoing preparation of the reports.

observations.recommendation: Analyze the entries made on the accounts affected from prior years to correct the abnormal balances and prepare necessary adjusting entries. Henceforth, prepare and maintain subsidiary records to facilitate application of payments against obligations and prepare the schedule of balances per creditor to support the general ledger balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implementation of COA’s recommendation is still ongoing. Trial balance for CY2016 shows the normal balance of the accounts but the agency has not yet prepared the subsidiary records and schedule of balances per creditor to support the GL balances.

observations.recommendation: Implement the projects embodied in the AIP and identify the reasons for the non-implementation of the programs and consequently make the necessary actions to facilitate the implementation of the programs/projects/ activities.
observations.managementAction: AAR Some projects embodied in the AIP were not yet implemented. Received the letter of explanation for the non-implementation of the said projects on February 25, 2015. Implementation is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to viable and sustainable programs, projects and activities that directly generate jobs and livelihood opportunities to its constituents in accordance with Department of Budget and Management and Department of Interior Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1, s. 2005 dated September 20, 2005 and Local Government Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and Section 287 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR Compliance is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Accomplish all the necessary data in the purchase orders to provide the necessary information and basis in determining the reliability of the procurement process in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010 and the Manual NGAS for LGUs. Likewise, copies of the purchase orders irrespective of amount including the supporting documents should be submitted to the auditor within five (5) days from the issuance thereof in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR Some purchase orders did not have the necessary information. Some purchase orders were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: Direct the accountant to submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the Bank Statements, credit/debit memos and paid checks for all the depository accounts of the City within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to determine the correctness of the cash in bank balance appearing in the financial statements and to avoid the imposition/filing of appropriate administrative sanctions for non-submission of required reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR Bank reconciliation Submission already done. The CY2016 bank reconciliation statements were submitted. Only snapshots can be provided since banks will submit their bank statements a month after the coverage period.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Accountant to substantiate the reconciling items presented in the BRS and prepare immediately the journal entry voucher/s for its adjustment.
observations.managementAction: AAR There were adjustments made by the Accounting Office totalling P446,739.60 covered by various JEV prepared during 2014.

observations.recommendation: Direct officials concerned to monitor and review the loan accounts, send demand letters to the borrowers and institute appropriate legal action against those who refuse to settle their obligations. Likewise, we recommend that Management demand an explanation from officials concerned for the granting of a loan to a city employee and impose administrative disciplinary action against them, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management collected the amount of P301,228.05 from Verdelita Montejo per JEV 2014-11-583. Demand letters were sent to employees with unliquidated cash advances; entities with loans payables and outstanding.

observations.recommendation: Direct the officials concerned to use the perpetual inventory method in recording drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies and as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs, and make the adjustments in the books, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: AAR The bookkeeping section had already started recording the procurement of drugs and medicines, medical, dental and laboratory supplies using the perpetual inventory method.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 34.2 of the RIRR of R.A. 9184 particularly in undertaking the post-qualification process to make certain that contracts shall be awarded only to bidders who have submitted valid and complete documents. Advise the concerned contractors to submit immediately the required documents.
observations.managementAction: AAR Documents were submitted to COA for the Road rehabilitation situated Barangay Awihao, Bato and Subayon, Toledo City in the total amount of P5,919,622.21. The projects were implemented by the JJ & J Construction.

observations.recommendation: Require the accounting department to adhere to the provision of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Manual, Volume I for LGUs in the recording of transactions. Direct the accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: AAR Documents submitted that the Road rehabilitation projects implemented by two contractors Alcobe and JJ Construction. Management has prepared three (3) JEV to record four (4) infrastructure projects accounts.

observations.recommendation: Require the refund of all honoraria claimed in violation of Budget Circular No. 2007-2 and henceforth, refrain from paying claims of this nature.

Management filed an appeal at the Commission on Audit Regional Office. Management filed an appeal which was later discontinued. observations.recommendation: Require the City’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: AAR For CY2013: There were no procurement made for the second semester of CY 2013. Submission of PMR was regularly done to GPPB until the first semester of 2016 due to transfer of employee in-charge to other department. A new employee was appointed. PMR will be submitted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the required supporting documents for evaluation within ninety (90) days after receipt of the notice of suspension, otherwise the suspension will become a disallowance. Require all disbursements by the City to be supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 or the State Auditing Code of the Philippines.

Total: P8,593,021.53. Management did not submit the required supporting documents to COA for the remaining P7,691,223.39.

observations.recommendation: Require the submission of duly filled-up drivers’ trip tickets to support the payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants totaling P5,696,357.37. Strictly enforce complete and proper accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline, oil and lubricants to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: AAR Some Driver’s Trip Tickets attached to the disbursement vouchers showing payment of gasoline, oil and lubricants from January to August 2014 did not indicate specific purpose, fuel usage/ balance in tank and distance travelled in kilometre. For CY2016, Management did not maintain Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels (MROT) during 2013-2014.

observations.recommendation: Exert effort on the titling of the 191 lots. Likewise, manpower and logistics should be allocated for the immediate transfer of title of these lots to the name of the City of Toledo.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Treasurer to avail of the civil remedies provided for in Chapter IV, Title I, Book II of R.A. 7160 and develop other feasible strategies to enhance collections of delinquent taxes from taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: AAR (no specific action text provided in table cell).

observations.recommendation: Require officials concerned to trace the causes of discrepancies and explain the variance of P16,614,549.32 in the inventory balance as of December 31, 2013. Direct the General Services and Accounting Departments to reconcile their records to establish the correct inventory balances. Strictly adhere to Section 114, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual. Henceforth, the City Accountant and the General Services Officer be required to maintain updated supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively. Require the City General Services Officer to conduct a physical inventory of supplies and materials every semester and submit the inventory reports thereon pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: AAR (no specific action text provided in table cell).

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to thoroughly review the journal entry vouchers (JEVs), and effect the corrections and adjustments to the Due From Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts. Likewise, he should closely supervise and promote coordination among the accounting personnel taking charge of General, Special Education and Trust Fund transactions to ensure completeness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR The reciprocal accounts affecting the Commercial Village and the General Fund were eliminated/ adjusted in CY2013.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accounting Department to record the purchases of drugs and medicine, medical, dental and laboratory supplies and office supplies, as well as other supplies and materials, under the inventory account and not directly charge it as expenses. Further, we recommend that the Chief Accountant maintain the perpetual inventory records comprising of the Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity/stock. The General Services Officer should likewise maintain stocks cards, the balance of which should be reconciled with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR Assigned one (1) staff to focus on the reconciliation of the Supplies account. Management did not maintain the Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity/ stock.

observations.recommendation: Exercise prudence in granting reimbursements for out of town travels. In instances when travel expenses exceed the P800.00 daily limit, we recommend that the official or employee attach a separate certification from the agency head as to the necessity of the expenses to the corresponding disbursement voucher to be reimbursed. Direct all concerned officials and employees to refund the above excess amount of per diems claimed otherwise, all those involved that authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable. Require officials and employees concerned to submit the required documents to this office to settle audit suspensions. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of Executive order No. 298, COA Circular 85-55A and pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: AAR Computation of per diems starting CY2013 were already in accordance with Executive Order Management stated that the accommodation as well as the equipment needed were provided, thus, the LGU was able to save from rental expenses of conference facilities. Management did not provide COA with copy of the official receipt showing the refund of the excess amount. Also, Management did not submit the required documents.
